title: rhinanthus mediterraneus subsp. arvernensis
intro: Rhinanthus mediterraneus subsp. arvernensis, also known as the Arvernian Yellow Rampion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family. This plant is a hemiparasitic perennial, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by drawing them from the roots of nearby plants. The medicinal properties of Rhinanthus mediterraneus subsp. arvernensis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures.
